name: Pico de Noez – circular via the Trenches Trail (PR-TO 23)
name_original: Pico de Noez – circular por el sendero de las Trincheras (PR-TO 23)
region_intro: Hiking around Noez offers access to the Sierra de Noez, a mountain range forming part of the Montes de Toledo. The landscape features Mediterranean vegetation, including holm oaks and rockroses, with numerous viewpoints. The area is characterized by rolling hills and the prominent Pico de Noez, which reaches an altitude of 1,034 meters. This region provides a natural setting for outdoor activities within the province of Toledo. Best hiking trails around Noez The most popular hiking route is Pico de Noez – circular via the Trenches Trail (PR-TO 23), a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular ascent to Pico de Noez with historical context. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico de Noez — round trip from Noez, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.5 km) path. This trail provides a direct ascent to the peak, offering panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38), an 8.5 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Noez is defined by the Sierra de Noez, Mediterranean vegetation, and numerous viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate ascents to longer trails. The routes in Noez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Noez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Noez?
A: Noez offers a variety of hiking trails, primarily within the Sierra de Noez, which is part of the Montes de Toledo. You'll find routes ranging from easy to difficult, with a strong focus on moderate ascents. The landscape is characterized by Mediterranean vegetation, including holm oaks and rockroses, and features numerous viewpoints offering impressive vistas.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Noez?
A: Yes, Noez has several options for less experienced hikers. There are 5 easy routes available, providing a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty without demanding significant effort. These trails are perfect for a leisurely walk amidst the Mediterranean flora.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Noez?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018786963/pico-de-noez-circular-via-the-trenches-trail-pr-to-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez – circular via the Trenches Trail (PR-TO 23)&lt;/a&gt; is a well-signposted and homologated route that offers an ideal experience for adventurous families. Its historical context, likely related to the Civil War, adds an interesting educational element to the hike.

Q: What is the highest point I can reach on a hike in Noez?
A: The highest point you can reach is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2675742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez&lt;/a&gt;, which stands at an altitude of 1,034 meters (approximately 3,392 feet). Several routes lead to the summit, offering stunning panoramic views of the surrounding area and the Montes de Toledo.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Noez?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018786963/pico-de-noez-circular-via-the-trenches-trail-pr-to-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez – circular via the Trenches Trail (PR-TO 23)&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the summit and back. Other options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19434177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez – Noez Peak loop from Noez&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez loop from Noez&lt;/a&gt;, providing varied experiences for hikers.

Q: What kind of natural features and landscapes will I encounter while hiking in Noez?
A: The landscape around Noez is defined by the Sierra de Noez and its Mediterranean vegetation. You'll encounter holm oaks (encinas), Spanish lavender (cantuesos), and rockroses (jaras). The area also features rolling hills and numerous viewpoints (miradores) that provide impressive vistas of the region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Noez?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the scenic beauty of the Sierra de Noez, the rewarding panoramic views from Pico de Noez, and the well-maintained trails like the Trenches Trail.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see along the trails in Noez?
A: While hiking in the Noez area, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018786963/pico-de-noez-circular-via-the-trenches-trail-pr-to-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trenches Trail (PR-TO 23)&lt;/a&gt; itself hints at historical significance, likely related to the Civil War. Additionally, nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadamur Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polán Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints in the Noez area?
A: The region around Noez is known for its various viewpoints (miradores) that offer impressive vistas. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2675742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the Sierra de Noez, provides particularly stunning panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes and the Montes de Toledo.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Noez?
A: There are 14 hiking routes available in Noez, offering a range of experiences for different skill levels. This includes 5 easy, 8 moderate, and 1 difficult trail, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to more experienced hikers.

Q: What is the 'Route of the Three Castles'?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019433717/route-of-the-three-castles-pr-to-38" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38)&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.5-mile (13.8 km) trail that leads through varied terrain. It's a moderate hike, typically completed in about 3 hours and 34 minutes, offering a journey through the historical landscape of the region.

Q: Are there any water features or reservoirs near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guajaraz Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable water feature in the vicinity of Noez. While not directly on every trail, it's an attraction that can be explored in conjunction with your hiking plans in the area.
tips: From its top at 1034 meters above sea level you can see beautiful views of the entire plain and the Montes de Toledo. Throughout the entire ascent route you will be able to enjoy some very picturesque panoramic views, the silhouette of the Sierra Layos to the northeast stands out.
